paper_metadata.title: GPT-NeoX-20B: An Open-Source Autoregressive Language Model
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce GPT-NeoX-20B, a 20 billion parameter autoregressive language model trained on the Pile, whose weights will be made freely and openly available to the public through a permissive license. It is, to the best of our knowledge, the largest dense autoregressive model that has publicly available weights at the time of submission. In this work, we describe \model{}'s architecture and training and evaluate its performance on a range of language-understanding, mathematics, and knowledge-based tasks. We find that GPT-NeoX-20B is a particularly powerful few-shot reasoner and gains far more in performance when evaluated five-shot than similarly sized GPT-3 and FairSeq models. We open-source the training and evaluation code, as well as the model weights, at https://github.com/EleutherAI/gpt-neox.

extraction_data.innovation: The primary innovation is the release of a 20 billion parameter autoregressive language model (GPT-NeoX-20B) with publicly available weights, which is the largest dense autoregressive language model with publicly available weights at the time of publication. This model is designed to enable open research in AI safety, mechanistic interpretability, and scaling of LLM capabilities.
extraction_data.application: Few-shot reasoning, language understanding, math, and knowledge-based tasks
extraction_data.research_problem: Open access to large language models for research in AI safety, mechanistic interpretability, and scaling of LLM capabilities
extraction_data.extension: Rotary positional embeddings applied to the first 25% of embedding vector dimensions, which twist the embedding spaces to make attention linearly dependent on the relative position (m-n).
